management_quotes.quote_text: For the first quarter of 2026, AMD expects revenue to be approximately $9.8 billion, plus or minus $300 million, including approximately $100 million of AMD Instinct MI308 sales to China. The mid-point of the revenue range represents year-over-year growth of approximately 32% and a sequential decline of approximately 5%. Non-GAAP gross margin is expected to be approximately 55%.

uncertainties.question: To what extent will further U.S. export control escalation restrict AMD's ability to sell current and next-generation AI GPU products to China, and how large is the total revenue exposure beyond the already-impacted MI308?
uncertainties.resolution: U.S. government announcements on export licensing policy; management disclosure in subsequent earnings calls of China-exposed GPU revenue as a percentage of total Data Center segment revenue

uncertainties.question: Can AMD sustain and expand operating margins as R&D and MG&A expenses grow at 39-41% YoY—well above revenue growth rates in some periods—while also fulfilling $25.7B in near-term purchase commitments?
uncertainties.resolution: Sequential quarters of non-GAAP operating margin data showing leverage from revenue scaling; management commentary on the trajectory and peak of investment cycle spending versus revenue inflection

uncertainties.question: Will the announced EPYC 'Venice' production ramp on TSMC 2nm translate into meaningful revenue contribution and continued server CPU share gains, or will supply constraints and customer qualification timelines delay the impact?
uncertainties.resolution: Management guidance quantifying Venice ramp revenue contribution; hyperscaler and OEM qualification announcements; TSMC 2nm yield and capacity disclosures

source_limitations.description: Only an earnings press release (SEC 8-K exhibit) was available. No earnings call transcript or Q&A session was acquired.
source_limitations.impact: Management tone and analyst question responses are absent.

catalyst_map.catalyst: Q1 2026 revenue guidance of ~$9.8B representing ~32% YoY growth, with sequential Data Center AI franchise expansion
catalyst_map.evidence_basis: Management guidance: 'For the first quarter of 2026, AMD expects revenue to be approximately $9.8 billion...The mid-point of the revenue range represents year-over-year growth of approximately 32%'

catalyst_map.catalyst: Production ramp of next-generation AMD EPYC 'Venice' processor on TSMC 2nm process technology announced May 21, 2026
catalyst_map.evidence_basis: Bear external evidence item: 'May 21, 2026 • 1:35 AM EDT. AMD Announces Production Ramp of Next-Generation AMD EPYC Processor Venice on TSMC 2nm Process Technology'

catalyst_map.catalyst: Expanded strategic partnership with Meta to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs, signaling large-scale hyperscaler adoption
catalyst_map.evidence_basis: Bear external evidence item: 'February 24, 2026 07:00 ET | Source: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. AMD and Meta Announce Expanded Strategic Partnership to Deploy 6 Gigawatts of AMD GPUs'

catalyst_map.catalyst: U.S. export control restrictions on AMD Instinct MI308 GPU sales to China, including ~$100M residual China sales in Q1 2026 guidance subject to further regulatory risk
catalyst_map.evidence_basis: Management risk_factors: 'Full year 2025 results included approximately $440 million in net inventory and related charges as a result of the U.S. Government's export control on AMD Instinct MI308 data center GPU products' and Q1 guidance including ~$100M MI308 China sales

catalyst_map.catalyst: Historically stronger second-half seasonality combined with rapid AI infrastructure spend scaling could drive revenue acceleration in H2 2026
catalyst_map.evidence_basis: MD&A: 'Historically, our net revenue has been generally higher in the second half of the year than in the first half of the year' and management commentary on 'accelerating adoption' and 'rapid scaling of data center AI franchise'

thesis_invalidation_conditions.condition: Further tightening of U.S. export controls that materially expands restrictions beyond MI308 to newer Instinct GPU generations, eliminating a significant addressable market and causing additional large inventory write-downs
thesis_invalidation_conditions.signal: New U.S. government licensing requirements or entity list additions covering MI350 or successor GPU products; management disclosure of incremental inventory charges beyond the $440M already recognized

thesis_invalidation_conditions.condition: Sustained gross margin compression below 50% driven by rising R&D (up 39% YoY) and MG&A costs (up 41% YoY) outpacing revenue growth, signaling deteriorating operating leverage
thesis_invalidation_conditions.signal: Non-GAAP gross margin guidance declining below 52% in consecutive quarters while operating expense growth continues to exceed revenue growth rates

thesis_invalidation_conditions.condition: Loss of significant CPU market share gains in Data Center (EPYC) or Client (Ryzen) segments, reversing the 51% YoY revenue growth trajectory that underpins the long-term compounder thesis
thesis_invalidation_conditions.signal: Sequential Data Center or Client segment revenue declines not explained by seasonality, accompanied by competitor announcements of design wins at key hyperscaler or OEM accounts

thesis_invalidation_conditions.condition: Failure to service or renegotiate $25.7B in unconditional purchase commitments (of which $18.3B are due in the remainder of FY2026) in a demand downturn, straining free cash flow and balance sheet
thesis_invalidation_conditions.signal: Management disclosure of demand shortfall relative to committed wafer/substrate purchases, leading to accelerating inventory build or write-downs beyond normal seasonal patterns
